deep_dive.title: End-to-End Parametric Portfolio Policies for Cross-Asset Futures Timing: When Do AI Models Beat Simple Rules?
deep_dive.abstract: Timing-based tilts across asset classes can drive much of the risk and return of a diversified cross-asset portfolio. The standard approach forecasts returns and then optimizes weights. We instead study an end-to-end AI-based policy that maps market states directly to portfolio weights, and we then ask when this one-step modeling approach outperforms simple rules-based strategies. We train these policies on the sixteen most liquid CME futures, where an edge is unlikely to be due to illiquidity, using a differentiable Sharpe ratio loss function, and we benchmark them against equal weighting, risk parity, and time-series momentum. The learned policies rank above the rules on the pooled cross-asset portfolio and in several sub-asset classes, but not uniformly. In gross terms, an LSTM and a transformer-based architecture perform comparably out-of-sample, but diverge when we consider transaction costs. The transformer generates the stronger learned policy, trades far less than the LSTM, and matches or exceeds equal weighting through moderate cost.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: end-to-end AI-based policy for cross-asset futures portfolio timing, directly mapping market states to portfolio weights
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 端到端AI策略直接映射市场状态到期货投资组合权重，用于跨资产择时
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 端到端AI策略直接映射市场状态到期货投资组合权重，用于跨资产择时
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 端到端AI策略直接映射市场状态到期货投资组合权重，用于跨资产择时
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: End-to-end AI portfolio policy trained with differentiable Sharpe ratio loss, outperforming simple rules on cross-asset futures timing.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 传统资产配置先预测收益再优化权重，本文直接学习从市场状态到组合权重的端到端AI策略。在16个最活跃CME期货上，使用可微夏普比率损失训练。该策略在跨资产组合和细分品类上均优于等权重、风险平价和时间序列动量等规则，表明端到端方法在复杂环境下更有效。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 传统资产配置先预测收益再优化权重，本文直接学习从市场状态到组合权重的端到端AI策略。在16个最活跃CME期货上，使用可微夏普比率损失训练。该策略在跨资产组合和细分品类上均优于等权重、风险平价和时间序列动量等规则，表明端到端方法在复杂环境下更有效。

deep_dive.title: Evidence-Supported Credit Risk Report Generation Using News-Centric Financial Knowledge Graphs
deep_dive.abstract: Financial markets evolve in response to real-world events reported in news, yet these drivers often remain implicit in text. To better explain market dynamics, event-market relations must be explicitly modeled through factual, company-centric, and environment-aware knowledge graphs. We present FinKG-News, a framework that automatically constructs such graphs by extracting news events as anchors linked to companies. Using FinKG-News as grounded evidence that integrates events, news, and company data, we develop an in-context learning architecture for credit risk report generation across three core financial dimensions. Automatic and human evaluations show that automated hallucination detection and quality assessment remain unreliable, making expert judgment indispensable. Our approach consistently outperforms baselines, improving quality by 19%-34% while reducing hallucinations. The source code and project resources are publicly available at: https://github.com/ichise-laboratory/FINKG-news.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: NLP for financial analysis: credit risk report generation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 自然语言处理用于金融分析：信用风险报告生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 自然语言处理用于金融分析：信用风险报告生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 自然语言处理用于金融分析：信用风险报告生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Uses news-centric financial knowledge graphs and in-context learning for credit risk report generation, directly applying NLP to financial analysis.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对金融新闻中事件与市场动态关联不明确的问题，提出了FinKG-News框架，自动构建以新闻事件为中心的金融知识图谱。利用该图谱作为证据，设计了一种上下文学习架构生成信用风险报告，涵盖三个核心金融维度。实验表明该方法能有效整合新闻事件与公司数据，但自动幻觉检测仍不可靠，展示了NLP在金融分析中的实际应用。

deep_dive.title: FinPersona-Bench: A Benchmark for Longitudinal Psychometric Stability of Autonomous Financial Agents
deep_dive.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed as autonomous financial agents initialized with explicit behavioral mandates such as "preserve capital" or "avoid speculative bets" that are meant to govern every decision throughout deployment. In practice, however, as market context accumulates over long horizons, these mandates gradually lose their behavioral influence, a phenomenon we formalize as Mandate Salience Decay (MSD). To measure MSD objectively, we introduce FinPersona-Bench, a simulation benchmark in which a synthetic market decouples observable price from hidden fundamental value, enabling falsifiable evaluation across three failure modes: trading without signal in calm markets, panic-selling during crashes, and ignoring fundamental value during speculative bubbles. Evaluating 18 leading frontier and open-source LLMs, each assigned one of three behavioral profiles ranging from strict capital preservation to aggressive growth, shows that MSD compounds over time and is model-dependent. In crash scenarios, the behavioral gap between static agents and those receiving periodic mandate re-grounding grows 4.4x from the first to the final quarter of the simulation. The effects of mandate re-grounding are not uniformly positive: it consistently helps conservative agents in low-signal markets but actively worsens behavior for aggressive agents in the same setting. These findings suggest that reliable long-horizon deployment requires selective, mandate-aware re-grounding based on agent profile and market regime.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for autonomous financial agents with behavioral mandates
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 用于评估自主金融代理行为稳定性的基准
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 用于评估自主金融代理行为稳定性的基准
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 用于评估自主金融代理行为稳定性的基准
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces FinPersona-Bench to evaluate LLMs as autonomous financial agents for mandate salience decay, using synthetic markets to test behavioral stability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 针对大语言模型作为自主金融代理时行为指令逐渐失效的问题，提出了FinPersona-Bench基准。该基准通过解耦市场价格与内在价值的合成市场，可证伪地评估三种失败模式（无信号交易、恐慌性抛售等）。实验表明代理在长期部署中存在指令显著性衰减现象。该工作为构建稳健的智能金融代理提供了评估框架。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 针对大语言模型作为自主金融代理时行为指令逐渐失效的问题，提出了FinPersona-Bench基准。该基准通过解耦市场价格与内在价值的合成市场，可证伪地评估三种失败模式（无信号交易、恐慌性抛售等）。实验表明代理在长期部署中存在指令显著性衰减现象。该工作为构建稳健的智能金融代理提供了评估框架。

deep_dive.title: Decision-focused Sparse Tangent Portfolio Optimization
deep_dive.abstract: Sparse tangent portfolio optimization aims to learn an interpretable, low-cardinality portfolio in the tangency direction of the mean-variance frontier. However, the associated cardinality-constrained formulation is NP-hard, and standard predict-then-optimize pipelines often misalign forecasting accuracy with downstream portfolio quality. We propose an end-to-end decision-focused learning framework that reformulates Sharpe ratio maximization as a Disciplined Parametrized Programming (DPP)-compliant convex programming layer and replaces discrete selection with a smooth top-$k$ operator enforcing an exact cardinality $k$. This enables gradient flow through prediction, asset selection, and re-optimization, allowing the predictive model to directly optimize portfolio performance. Across four major equity markets, our method achieves competitive and often superior out-of-sample Sharpe ratios compared with historical and prediction-focused baselines, with particularly strong gains in larger asset universes. Our \href{https://github.com/feuerwerksh/Diffble-card-SR}{code} is publicly available.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: deep learning portfolio optimization for stock market
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 深度学习用于股票市场投资组合优化
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 深度学习用于股票市场投资组合优化
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 深度学习用于股票市场投资组合优化
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: End-to-end decision-focused learning for sparse tangent portfolio optimization, directly optimizing portfolio performance.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对稀疏切线投资组合优化中的NP-hard问题，提出了端到端决策聚焦学习框架。通过将夏普比率最大化转化为凸规划层，并使用平滑top-k算子实现精确基数约束，使梯度流经预测、资产选择和再优化。该方法直接优化投资组合性能，避免了传统预测-优化流程中的对齐问题，适用于股票市场分析。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 该论文针对稀疏切线投资组合优化中的NP-hard问题，提出了端到端决策聚焦学习框架。通过将夏普比率最大化转化为凸规划层，并使用平滑top-k算子实现精确基数约束，使梯度流经预测、资产选择和再优化。该方法直接优化投资组合性能，避免了传统预测-优化流程中的对齐问题，适用于股票市场分析。

deep_dive.title: Shapley in Context: Explaining Financial Language with Domain Expertise
deep_dive.abstract: In recent years, large language models have achieved remarkable success and have seen growing adoption in financial applications. At the same time, explainability remains critical in finance, a domain characterized by high stakes and strict regulatory requirements. Although numerous methods have been proposed to explain black box machine learning models, the majority of these approaches are designed for general purpose tasks and do not incorporate domain specific knowledge. In this work, we study the explainability of financial textual data modeled by large language models through the lens of the Shapley value. Specifically, we investigate whether Shapley based attributions align with established financial domain knowledge. Through rigorous theoretical analysis and extensive empirical evaluations, we demonstrate that Shapley values can yield explanations that are consistent with financial reasoning and can offer meaningful insights into the model's behavior in text based financial applications.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: explaining financial language with Shapley values and domain expertise
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 使用Shapley值和领域知识解释金融语言模型
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 使用Shapley值和领域知识解释金融语言模型
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 使用Shapley值和领域知识解释金融语言模型
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper studies whether Shapley value attributions align with financial domain knowledge when explaining LLM predictions on financial text, finding that domain-specific context improves interpretability.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 研究了Shapley值在解释金融文本大语言模型预测时是否与金融领域知识一致。通过理论分析和实验，发现结合领域特定上下文的归因方法能够更好地对齐专家知识，从而提升可解释性。这项工作对于金融领域的透明度和监管合规具有重要意义。

quick_skim.title: Agent-to-Agent Finance: Blockchain Payments and Trust Infrastructure for Autonomous AI Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Autonomous AI agents are beginning to occupy a position between analytical tools and transacting counterparties. They can interpret goals, call external tools, negotiate with other agents, access data and computation, and in some settings initiate payments or blockchain transactions. This development creates a distinct problem for financial markets: if software agents can act economically, market participants need infrastructure for identity, authorisation, payment, verification, reputation and accountability. This article develops the concept of agent-to-agent finance as the layer of machine-mediated financial interaction in which autonomous agents discover counterparties, purchase services, express transaction intent, execute payments and generate auditable evidence. The argument is not that blockchain is a universal substrate for finance, but that programmable settlement, smart wallets, decentralised registries and verifiable computation can address specific coordination frictions created by autonomous agents. Drawing on recent work on blockchain A2A payments, ERC-8004 agent registries, provenance-based wallets, deterministic inference, DeFi intent mining, and official evidence on AI adoption in financial services, the article situates agent-to-agent finance as an emerging form of financial market infrastructure. It argues that the decisive design question is bounded autonomy: how to let agents transact without making markets more opaque, fragile or unaccountable.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: infrastructure for autonomous AI agent financial transactions
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 自主AI代理金融交易的基础设施
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 自主AI代理金融交易的基础设施

quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对自主AI代理在金融市场中作为交易对手方的新角色，提出了代理对代理金融（Agent-to-Agent Finance）的概念。文章系统阐述了代理身份、授权、支付、验证、声誉和问责等基础设施需求。该框架为机器中介的金融交互提供了理论支撑，涵盖代理发现、服务购买、交易意图表达等环节。该工作为AI代理融入金融体系奠定了基础设施层面的基础。

quick_skim.title: Neural Network-Based Estimation of Time-Dependent Parameters in AR(p) Processes
quick_skim.abstract: We investigate a forecasting framework based on a simple discrete-time dynamic model with coefficients varying in time. The parameters of the model are recovered within a deep learning framework, which makes it possible to retain a transparent parametric structure while simultaneously accounting for complex and nonstationary patterns in the observed phenomenon. Our analysis covers two specifications of the noise process. Besides the standard Gaussian setting, we also consider Laplace-distributed noise, which can offer a more adequate description in the presence of heavier tails and sharper local fluctuations. For both cases, we formulate the predictive scheme of the model and analyze the associated uncertainty quantification, including the construction of prediction intervals. The results illustrate that a relatively simple model, when combined with time-dependent parameter estimation, can serve as a mathematically tractable and practically flexible tool for forecasting complex dynamics under different noise assumptions. The general model is stated for TVAR($p$), while the prediction-interval formulas and the numerical experiments are developed for the TVAR(1) case.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: neural network for time-dependent AR parameters applicable to financial forecasting
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 神经网络估计时变AR参数，可应用于金融预测

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 神经网络估计时变AR参数，可应用于金融预测
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: This paper proposes a deep learning framework to estimate time-varying parameters in AR(p) processes, demonstrating effective forecasting and uncertainty quantification under Gaussian and Laplace noise.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 提出一种基于深度学习的框架来估计自回归模型中时变参数，允许模型保留透明参数结构同时捕捉复杂非平稳模式。在标准高斯噪声和厚尾拉普拉斯噪声下均给出了预测方案和不确定性量化，表明该方法在金融时间序列分析中具有潜在应用价值。
